name: Transformation2d
summary: (No description provided in vendor docs for Geom::Transformation2d.)

constructors.name: initialize
constructors.summary: The #initialize method creates a new Geom::Transformation2d. You can use this method or one of the more specific methods for creating specific kinds of Geom::Transformation2d.
constructors.remarks: The #initialize method creates a new Geom::Transformation2d. You can use this method or one of the more specific methods for creating specific kinds of Geom::Transformation2d.

methods.name: rotation
methods.summary: The rotation method is used to create a transformation that does rotation about a point.
methods.remarks: The rotation method is used to create a transformation that does rotation about a point.

methods.name: scaling
methods.signature: scaling(scale) => Geom::Transformation2d | scaling(xscale, yscale) => Geom::Transformation2d | scaling(point, scale) => Geom::Transformation2d | scaling(point, xscale, yscale) => Geom::Transformation2d
methods.summary: The scaling method is used to create a transformation that does scaling.
methods.remarks: The scaling method is used to create a transformation that does scaling.

params.name: scale
params.doc: The global scale factor for the transform.

methods.name: translation
methods.summary: The translation method is used to create a transformation that does translation.
methods.remarks: The translation method is used to create a transformation that does translation.

methods.summary: The #* method is used to do matrix multiplication using the transform.
methods.remarks: The #* method is used to do matrix multiplication using the transform.

methods.name: clone
methods.summary: The #clone method creates a copy of the Geom::Transformation2d.
methods.remarks: The #clone method creates a copy of the Geom::Transformation2d.

methods.name: identity?
methods.summary: The #identity? method determines if the Geom::Transformation2d is the IDENTITY_2D transform.
methods.remarks: The #identity? method determines if the Geom::Transformation2d is the IDENTITY_2D transform.

returns.doc: true if the transform is the identity

methods.name: inverse
methods.summary: The #inverse method is used to retrieve the inverse of a transformation.
methods.remarks: The #inverse method is used to retrieve the inverse of a transformation.

methods.name: invert!
methods.summary: The #invert! method sets the transformation to its inverse.
methods.remarks: The #invert! method sets the transformation to its inverse.

methods.name: set!
methods.summary: The #set! method sets the Geom::Transformation2d to match another one.
methods.remarks: The #set! method sets the Geom::Transformation2d to match another one. The argument is anything that can be converted into a Geom::Transformation2d.

methods.name: to_a
methods.summary: The #to_a method returns a 6 element array which contains the values that define the Transformation2d.
methods.remarks: The #to_a method returns a 6 element array which contains the values that define the Transformation2d.

properties.summary: The #== method checks to see if the two Geom::Transformation2ds are equal.
properties.remarks: The #== method checks to see if the two Geom::Transformation2ds are equal. This checks whether the values of the transformations are the same.
